title: [Feraligatr] Battle Guide
summary: A [Water] physical setup sweeper whose [sheer-force] ability converts [liquidation], [crunch], and [ice-punch] into 1.3x boosted finishers after a [dragon-dance].
strengths: [sheer-force] boosts every secondary-effect move by 30 percent
[dragon-dance] solves the Speed tier problem in one turn
Broad cove[rage] pool — [Ghost], [Dragon], [Ground], and [Psychic] all covered
weaknesses: 78 Speed is too slow without a [dragon-dance] up
2x [Grass] and [Electric] weaknesses threaten the setup turn
Priority attackers easily interrupt the setup

recommendedMoves.reason: Critical setup for both Attack and Speed

recommendedItems.reason: Boosts [Water] STAB damage with no drawback
Absorbs one status move to secure the setup turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Feraligatr] is a [Water]-type physical setup sweeper whose entire plan revolves a[round] the [sheer-force] hidden ability plus [dragon-dance]. Base 105 Attack and 78 Speed look mediocre at first, but [sheer-force] boosts every secondary-effect move ([liquidation], [crunch], [ice-punch]) by 30 percent, and a [sing]le dance turns those into game-ending finishers. In the Champions format [Feraligatr] is a reliable late-game sweeper that closes out matches once the oppo[sing] walls have been weakened.

## Strengths
[sheer-force] is the damage scaler. [liquidation], [crunch], and [ice-punch] all qualify, so [Feraligatr]'s effective power is considerably higher than its raw stats suggest. [dragon-dance] solves the Speed tier in one turn. The cove[rage] trio of [Water], [Dark], and [Ice] covers almost every wall in the format, making [Feraligatr] difficult to switch into after setup.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 78 Speed is the main structural issue. 2x [Grass] and [Electric] weaknesses mean any special attacker running those types threatens to end the setup turn. Priority attackers — [extreme-speed], [aqua-jet] from Scizor-style users.

## Recommended Moveset
[liquidation] is the locked [Water] STAB because [sheer-force] boosts it and it has a defense-lowering secondary that's a meaningful threat mid-sweep. [crunch] is the mandatory [Dark] move for [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls. [ice-punch] covers [Grass], [Dragon], and [Ground] attackers that would otherwise wall the [Water] STAB. [dragon-dance] fills the fourth slot. [earthquake] is an alternative for [Electric] cove[rage] if the team needs it.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[mystic-water] is a clean [Water] STAB booster with no drawbacks and doesn't lock moves, making it the default for the four-attack set. [lum-berry] is the alternate that [absorb]s one status attempt and guarantees the setup turn. The ability must be [sheer-force]. Tera [Water] maxes [liquidation] damage, and Tera [Dark] turns the setup mid-sweep into a [crunch]-oriented cleaner with extra cove[rage] resistance.

## Teammates & Synergy
The biggest structural need is [Grass] and [Electric] cove[rage]. A slow pivot creates the entry turns [Feraligatr] needs to dance, and an [intimidate] user on the back line reduces the priority pressure that would otherwise interrupt the setup. Hazard removal also matters for [Feraligatr]'s bulk over a long game.

## Play Patterns
[Feraligatr] wants a late-game setup slot. Enter on a predicted [Water]-weak switch, dance on the swap turn, and start chaining cove[rage] attacks. Use [crunch] against [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls, [ice-punch] for [Dragon]s and [Grass]es, and [liquidation] for everything neutral. Save the Tera for the moment a [Grass] or [Electric] move is confirmed.
